fields.label: Target kind
fields.rationale: DocType is the common case (per-document layout). Report formats render Report Builder output; they auto-set custom_format=1 because Report output cannot be templated via the visual Print Format Builder.

fields.label: Target DocType (when print_format_for='DocType')
fields.rationale: Required when print_format_for='DocType'. Empty when print_format_for='Report'.

fields.label: Target Report (when print_format_for='Report')
fields.rationale: Required when print_format_for='Report'. The controller rejects the save if empty in that case.

fields.label: Custom (non-Builder) format
fields.rationale: When 1, the print format is hand-authored (Jinja HTML or raw printer commands) - the html or raw_commands field is required. When 0, the format uses the visual Print Format Builder (drag-and-drop). Report formats force custom_format=1 at save.

fields.label: Template type (when custom_format=1)
fields.rationale: Jinja is the canonical path: validate_template() checks syntax at save. JS is deprecated (legacy JavaScript-rendered formats); avoid unless explicitly required. Ignored when custom_format=0.

fields.label: Raw printing (thermal / label printers)
fields.rationale: When 1, bypass HTML rendering and use raw_commands (native printer commands like ESC/POS, ZPL). Used for thermal receipts and label printers. When 0, the html field is used. Mutually exclusive with html being present.

fields.label: Jinja HTML body (when !raw_printing)
fields.rationale: Required when custom_format=1 AND raw_printing=0. Full Jinja HTML referencing `doc.<field>`. The Jinja context is ONLY the doc object plus a small set of helpers - no frappe.session, no arbitrary frappe.* calls. Use doc.get_formatted('field') for currency / date formatting. validate_template checks syntax at save.

fields.label: Raw printer commands (when raw_printing=1)
fields.rationale: Required when raw_printing=1. Native printer language commands (ESC/POS for thermal receipts, ZPL for Zebra labels). Jinja-rendered on the server side like html but output straight to the printer.

fields.label: Standard
fields.rationale: 'No' = site-local (DB-only). 'Yes' writes to an app's filesystem and requires developer_mode AND the caller cannot be in migrate / install / test context - the controller rejects saves of standard formats outside those explicit flows.

fields.label: Default for DocType
fields.rationale: Default 0. When 1, this format replaces the DocType's built-in default print format. Setting default=1 on a new format does NOT auto-clear default=1 on existing formats - the user sees two 'defaults' until one is manually cleared.

fields.label: Letter head
fields.rationale: Optional. Names an existing Letter Head whose content / footer wraps this print format. Empty means no letter head wrapper.

fields.label: PDF generator
fields.rationale: wkhtmltopdf is the default and works for most layouts. chrome uses headless Chromium and renders modern CSS better (flexbox, grid) but is slower and has a heavier runtime dependency.

fields.label: Disabled
fields.rationale: Default enabled. Disabled formats are hidden from the Print dialog but kept in the DB for restoration.

fields.label: Top margin (mm)
fields.rationale: Page margin in millimetres. Frappe default 15mm fits most business documents. Lower margins (5-10mm) for dense invoices; raise for letterhead-only print formats.

fields.label: Bottom margin (mm)
fields.rationale: Bottom margin; must accommodate the footer content. Default 15mm.

fields.label: Left margin (mm)
fields.rationale: Left margin. Default 15mm. Asymmetric margins (e.g. larger left for binding) are common for printed reports.

fields.label: Right margin (mm)
fields.rationale: Right margin. Default 15mm.

fields.label: Google Font name
fields.rationale: Optional. Names a Google Font to load in the rendered HTML (e.g. 'Inter', 'Roboto'). Empty uses the system default (usually Helvetica / Arial fallback). Not applied when custom_format=1 + print_format_type='Jinja' with inline styling.

fields.label: Base font size (pt)
fields.rationale: Base point size for body text. Default 14pt. Lower (10-12pt) for dense reports; raise for accessibility.

fields.label: Additional CSS
fields.rationale: Custom CSS rules appended to the rendered print HTML. Use for brand styling (borders, colours, spacing) that the Jinja template doesn't express inline. Empty for unstyled defaults.

fields.label: Right-align field labels
fields.rationale: Cosmetic: when 1, field labels align to the right of their columns. Common in RTL layouts and formal letterhead styles.

fields.label: Show section break headings
fields.rationale: When 1, Section Break labels render as printed headings. Useful for long documents where section boundaries help readers; noise for short invoices.

fields.label: Show negatives as positive (for credit notes)
fields.rationale: Niche: when 1, negative values in Currency / Quantity / Count fields render as their absolute value. Used on Credit Note / refund print formats where the negativity is implicit in the document type, not shown in the number.
